description_long: This object is an ornately decorated shotgun with a wooden body featuring intricate carved patterns and a weathered, aged finish. The stock is rounded and bulbous, adorned with swirling motifs that extend along the grip and receiver. The barrel is long and straight, with a metallic appearance and subtle patina, suggesting age and frequent use. The trigger and trigger guard are visible, with the guard being rounded and integrated into the design. The hammer and firing mechanism are exposed, with a classic flintlock or percussion cap style. The overall appearance is that of a historical or antique shotgun, likely intended for display or period reenactment in an urban setting.
description: An ornately carved, antique-style shotgun with a rounded wooden stock, long barrel, and exposed firing mechanism, featuring a weathered finish and intricate decorative patterns.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the shotgun, highlighting the ornate carvings on the stock and grip, as well as the exposed hammer and trigger guard.
description_view_1: Front-on view down the barrel, emphasizing the rounded stock and the alignment of the barrel, with minimal detail visible on the sides.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the other profile of the shotgun, with similar decorative carvings and the visible trigger and hammer mechanism.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the rounded end of the stock and the grip, with the barrel extending away and little detail visible on the firing mechanism.
attribute_barrel_configuration: single-barrel, no visible ribbing or venting, long barrel
attribute_trigger_guard_design: rounded, medium-sized trigger guard with no visible finger grooves or reinforcement
